short: AI systems released with open code and, often, open model weights.
definition: Open source AI refers to AI systems released with open code and, in many cases, open model weights (the trained numbers that make up a model), so that others can inspect, run, and modify them. The exact meaning varies — some releases share training code and data, others share only the trained weights under a license. This openness supports independent research, local deployment, and adaptation without depending on a single provider.
